pages.text_excerpt: News | Dr Neil Hudson Skip to main content Dr Neil Hudson Member of Parliament for Epping Forest Main navigation About Neil News My Campaigns In Parliament Contact Dr Neil Hudson Member of Parliament for Epping Forest News Epping Forest MP meets with Health Minister to press for a Chigwell GP Surgery Friday, 1 May, 2026 Dr Neil Hudson, Member of Parliament for Epping Forest, has reaffirmed his ongoing campaign to secure a dedicated GP surgery for residents of Chigwell following a constructive meeting this week with Health Minister Stephen Kinnock and senior staff members of the new Essex ICB. Chigwell remains the only parish in the Epping Forest constituency without its own GP practice, despite a population of... Caring More for Our Mental Health I passionately believe that we need parity of esteem between physical and mental health, and I will fight so folk have better access to mental health provision... Protecting Green Spaces, Agriculture and the Environment Here in Epping Forest, we are so fortunate to have wonderful green spaces and our beautiful and precious Forest. The Central Line is a vital artery for us – from... deliver the vital services relied on for workers and employers in Epping Forest on issues like healthcare and transport services.In his speech in the 202 Veterinary MP shows his support for assistance invaluable lifeline they provide for those who need on them, at a cross party Parliamentary event.The event, hosted by Assistance Dogs UK (ADUK), Assista Epping Forest MP repeats calls for Government greater awareness of sepsis and its signs.Sepsis is life-threatening Shadow Environment Minister tells Government: Tackle Water Company Borrowing Thursday, 11 September, 2025 In the House of Commons, Shadow Environment Minister Dr Neil Hudson MP urged the Labour Government to save consumers and the taxpayer from the fallout of financial issues of companies like Thames Water, by introducing limits on their borrowing levels, during Oral Parliamentary Questions to the Depar Dr Neil Hudson MP joins the Farm Safety Foundation to promote Farm Safety Week in Parliament Tuesday, 22 July, 2025 Shadow DEFRA Minister Dr Neil Hudson MP took the opportunity in Parliament to champion Farm Safety Week, meeting with the Farm Safety Foundation (Yellow Wellies), to urgently call for change to keep our farmers safe at their parliamentary event in advance of Farm Safety Week.Farm Safety Week is an a Dr Neil Hudson MP celebrates campaign win with 3 Dads Walking on suicide prevention in the school curriculum Wednesday, 16 July, 2025 This week, Dr Neil Hudson, MP for Epping Forest, celebrated the major campaign win with the 3 Dads Walking to see safe, age-appropriate lessons on suicide prevention be introduced in the secondary school curriculum in England.Just this week, the Government published new statutory guidance for school Epping Forest MP champions vital access to pages.text_excerpt: About RSS | Dr Neil Hudson Skip to main content Dr Neil Hudson Member of Parliament for Epping Forest Main navigation About Neil News My Campaigns In Parliament Contact Dr Neil Hudson Member of Parliament for Epping Forest About RSS RSS feeds and news syndication News feeds Events feeds You can read the latest news from this website using your news reader, or display them on your own website using RSS (Rich Site Summary) XML documents, wherever you see the orange RSS symbol. What is RSS? RSS is a defacto XML standard invented by Netscape for news distribution, now used by thousands of sites. RSS is an open format that uses a simple set of XML tags to define a news "channel" that allows any site to provide basic meta-information about your new content (headline, link, description). Whenever you use our RSS feeds publicly (where anyone but yourself would be reading them), you should provide proper attribution to this website. We cannot provide advice or technical support in the coding and integration of our RSS feeds into your site or software. Additionally we ask that you cache the RSS feed and do not query more than once in any hour.
